ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific entry for slow-roasted lamb leg with gravy and vegetables; closest parent 'lamb food product' used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC represents cooked sheep/lamb meat; no specific code for slow-roasted lamb leg with accompaniments exists.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 227197003 represents lamb as a food substance; no specific code for the prepared dish exists.

serving.description: 1 plate (lamb with gravy, salad, and butter rice or bread)
serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 450 g, comprising a portion of slow-roasted lamb leg (around 200 g), gravy, roasted carrots and potatoes, salad, and a side of butter rice or bread.

nutrient_source.title: Lamb, leg, whole (shank and sirloin), separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/4 inch fat, cooked, roasted — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 17086)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are based on USDA SR Legacy data for roasted lamb leg (separable lean and fat, trimmed to 1/4 inch fat, cooked, roasted). The dish as served includes accompaniments (carrots, potatoes, gravy, salad, butter rice or bread); per-100g values reflect an estimated composite at approximately 55% lamb, 20% potato/carrot, 15% gravy, 10% salad ratio.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for slow-roasted lamb leg as a composite dish. The GI of 38 is an analogous estimate derived from the mixed-meal composition: lamb meat has a negligible GI (protein/fat dominant, GI ~0), roasted potatoes have a GI of approximately 70–80 (University of Sydney GI Database), cooked carrots GI ~39, and butter rice GI ~56. The composite dish GI is estimated at approximately 38 (low) based on the weighted carbohydrate contribution of each component. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (38 × 38.25) / 100 ≈ 14.5, based on 450 g serving with 8.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from gravy and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: The dish contains approximately 280 mg sodium per 100 g, primarily from gravy and herb seasoning; individuals with hypertension should request reduced-gravy portions and monitor total sodium intake, as a full 450 g serving may contribute approximately 1260 mg sodium, representing over 50% of the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate load from potato and rice or bread accompaniment
type2_diabetes.guidance: The composite dish has a low estimated glycemic index of 38, but the glycemic load per full serving is approximately 14.5 (moderate); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should choose the salad side over butter rice or bread to reduce the carbohydrate load, and limit portion size of potatoes to manage postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from lamb and gravy
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Roasted lamb leg contains approximately 3.8 g saturated fat and 72 mg cholesterol per 100 g; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size to 150–200 g of the lamb component, choose lean cuts, and request gravy on the side to reduce saturated fat intake in line with AHA dietary guidelines.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tender Roasted Lamb is a Middle Eastern-inspired slow-roasted lamb leg dish served at Sahara Bar & Restaurant in Singapore. The lamb leg is slow-roasted until tender with carrots and potatoes, seasoned with a blend of aromatic herbs typical of Middle Eastern cuisine such as cumin, coriander, and rosemary. The dish is served with a rich meat gravy, a fresh salad, and a choice of butter rice or bread. The lamb is characteristically tender with a slightly caramelised exterior, rich umami flavour, and a moist, succulent texture from the slow-roasting process. Nutritionally, the dish is a high-protein meal providing approximately 16.8 g protein per 100 g, with moderate fat content (9.2 g per 100 g) including saturated fat from the lamb. The carbohydrate content varies depending on the chosen accompaniment (butter rice or bread). The dish is a good source of vitamin B12, zinc, iron, and selenium from the lamb. Clinically, the sodium content from gravy warrants attention for hypertension management, and the carbohydrate accompaniment choice is relevant for Type 2 Diabetes management. The dish is suitable for halal diets and is a culturally significant offering in Singapore's Middle Eastern restaurant scene.
common_user_queries: How many calories in roasted lamb?
Is roasted lamb good for diabetics?

confidence_reason: No exact match for this composite dish exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy data for roasted lamb leg (the primary component) with estimated contributions from accompaniments. The composite per-100g values are a reasonable approximation but carry inherent uncertainty due to variable accompaniment ratios and restaurant-specific preparation. GI is an analogous estimate from approved GI sources. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Per-100g nutrient values represent an estimated composite of the full dish (approximately 55% lamb, 20% potato/carrot, 15% gravy, 10% salad) based on USDA SR Legacy data for roasted lamb leg (NDB 17086) as the primary anchor, supplemented by USDA data for roasted potatoes, cooked carrots, and generic gravy. 2. The dish is served with a choice of butter rice or bread; the per-100g values assume butter rice as the default accompaniment for the composite estimate. Choosing bread instead of butter rice would alter the carbohydrate and fat profile. 3. Sodium content is estimated to be higher than the lamb-only value due to gravy and seasoning; the 280 mg/100g estimate reflects the composite dish. 4. All numeric micronutrient values (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for roasted lamb leg and are approximate for the composite dish. No -1 sentinel values are used in this record. 5. GI is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this composite dish was found in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The dish is served at Sahara Bar & Restaurant, 58 Boat Quay, Singapore, sourced from field survey data. 7. Multilingual names provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this dish is served in Singapore. No Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, or Vietnamese names are required as this is a Middle Eastern dish served in a Singapore context. 8. Aliases consistency verified: all strings in aliases_by_language (english, chinese, malay, tamil, hindi, romanized) appear verbatim in the flat aliases list.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Built full composite nutrient profile from USDA SR Legacy data for roasted lamb leg as primary anchor. Added estimated contributions from accompaniments (potatoes, carrots, gravy, salad).

Iteration 2: Verified aliases_by_language consistency with flat aliases list. Confirmed all Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi strings appear verbatim in flat aliases. Verified all ontology match_type values use only approved strings (exact, closest_parent, none). Confirmed no -1 sentinel values remain in per_100g. Confirmed GI is numeric and > 0.

Iteration 3: Root cause of q8 failure identified as mixed-script corruption in the Hindi alias within aliases_by_language ('भुना हुआ मेmனா' contained Tamil character 'ன' and Latin 'm' instead of pure Devanagari). Corrected aliases_by_language hindi entry to 'भुना हुआ मेमना' (pure Devanagari). Verified flat aliases list also uses the identical corrected string 'भुना हुआ मेमना'. Performed full character-level consistency audit across all language keys in aliases_by_language against flat aliases — all 15 entries confirmed consistent.
